title: de-opinionate template layout: drop core/viewer layers, single package, arch-fitness layer-agnostic
description: Scaffold was shipping the 3-repo core->trainer->viewer layering as imposed structure. Guardrails must be layout-agnostic. Drop core/ + viewer layer + has_viewer/viewer_name/viewer_imports_trainer toggles. Ship ONE package {{ package_name }} (two internal modules: math_ops<-pipeline, keeps an import edge + astgrep-compliant). Remove import-linter from defaults (directional contracts are project-specific; graph.py covers cycles) -> document as opt-in. graph.py --assert stays as the layer-agnostic arch gate (god/cycle/file-size). Update pyproject/noxfile/ci/pre-commit/conftest/test_e2e/copier.yml/docs. Re-green E2E.

title: refresh docs/SPEC.md to post-refactor state (packages list, ship_example, no viewer/import-linter)
description: SPEC.md is pre-refactor: toggle table still lists package_name/has_viewer/viewer_name/viewer_imports_trainer; layer model core<trainer<viewer; import-linter as shipped gate family 5. Post-refactor truth: packages (comma-list) + computed package_name + ship_example; no imposed layering; import-linter is opt-in not shipped; graph.py is the layer-agnostic arch gate; ruff_select + tool versions single-sourced (when:false). Update the toggle table, drop the layer-model section (replace with packages-list + opt-in contracts note), fix the gate-family table (remove import-linter row or mark opt-in), align with the new README. Root README points here.

title: ship_example toggle: make the demo package (math_ops/pipeline) optional for repo adoption
description: The example package math_ops<-pipeline + its unit tests exist so a fresh generation has code to lint/test/graph. When an EXISTING repo adopts the scaffold (copier copy with package_name=their package), the stub collides with / is noise beside their real code and must be manually deleted. Add a ship_example bool toggle (default true). When false: exclude {{ package_name }}/math_ops.py, pipeline.py, tests/unit/test_math_ops.py, test_pipeline.py via _exclude, so adoption yields guardrails-only. Document both paths (new vs adopt) in README. Keep coverage floor sane when example absent (nothing to cover -> floor still passes on empty or the repo's own tests).

title: AUDIT (ext/mindscape): template graph.py missing cardioseg's test-mirror sub-rule
description:
External audit by the mindscape agent while checking devtools sync (mindscape will consume this scaffold).

FINDING: template/devtools/graph.py assert_fitness blocks on god-module + cycle + god-file only. It does NOT carry cardioseg's test-mirror rule — cardioseg/devtools/graph.py defines unmirrored() and does 'blocking += [test mirror ...] for m in unmirrored(packages)' (its graph.py line ~146, GRADUATED to blocking bd j4m2). LEARNINGS.md says the scaffold 'faithfully mirrors cardioseg (most-mature)', but this sub-rule was dropped. So a generated project (and mindscape on consume) loses a gate cardioseg enforces.

IMPACT: mindscape just adopted this rule (its bd 888) as a STRICT path-mirror. Consuming the scaffold as-is would regress it. Both mature siblings (cardioseg + mindscape) enforce test-mirror; the scaffold is the outlier.

SUGGESTED FIX (if accepted): port unmirrored() into template/devtools/graph.py behind enforce_arch_fitness. Use mindscape's more-evolved variant (the superset): strict path-mirror — a logic module <pkg>/<path>/foo.py is mirrored iff tests/unit/<path>/test_foo.py EXISTS — PLUS a coverage-omit shell exemption (unmirrored() reads [tool.coverage] omit; runners/adapters/GPU/download/viz glue exempt so non-unit-testable shells don't force stub tests). Cardioseg exempts only __init__/__main__ (few shells); mindscape's omit carve generalizes better for projects with many shells. Render the [tool.coverage] omit into the generated pyproject via copier. Reference impls: cardiac-seg/devtools/graph.py (strict, minimal) and mindscape/devtools/graph.py (strict + omit exemption).

NOTE: also confirmed the reverse direction is fine — mindscape LACKS the 3 class-shape smell explorers (lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates) the scaffold ships; that is the intended superset mindscape will adopt on consume, not a scaffold defect.

title: refactor: align test-dir naming (end_to_end vs e2e)
description: Scaffold's own suite is tests/end_to_end/; template ships tests/e2e/. Pick one convention across scaffold + template.

title: refactor: declutter scaffold root into docs/
description: Move scaffold's own docs (SPEC.md, LEARNINGS.md) into a docs/ dir; keep README.md + copier.yml at root. Reduces root noise (currently ~11 root files).

title: refactor: single-source pinned tool versions
description: ruff@0.15.13 / vulture@2.16 / nox / pre-commit versions are duplicated across ci.yml, noxfile, pre-commit, e2e.yml, conftest. Single-source via copier.yml computed vars (when:false, like ruff_select) so a bump is one edit.

title: refactor: clean conditional filenames -> copier _exclude
description: Replace all 7 '{% if toggle %}name{% endif %}' conditional filenames/dirs with CLEAN names + jinja _exclude rules in copier.yml (copier renders _exclude with answers). Targets: viewer dir + tests/viewer, devtools sgconfig/sg-rules/jscpd, class-shape trio (lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates -> real .py names). BONUS: real .py extensions let ruff format the trio directly, killing the copy-back hack. VERIFY copier _exclude supports jinja first. Keep {{ package_name }}/{{ viewer_name }} as clean templated names.

title: enable_beads copier toggle (future)
description: Generated projects get bd init + the CLAUDE/AGENTS beads section. All 3 real repos use beads; scaffold does not ship it. Not in the current testing goal.

title: scaffold CI runs tests/unit too
description: e2e.yml: uv run pytest covers tests/unit + tests/end_to_end. Full pytest green in CI.

title: devtools/README.md shipped in template
description: template/devtools/README.md.jinja, toggle-aware: one line per shipped tool (graph/lcom/data_clumps/state_candidates + astgrep/jscpd when on): what it detects + how to run.

title: graph.py --assert inject-test in E2E
description: Parallel to astgrep/jscpd inject tests: write a god-module/cycle into a generated project -> assert graph --assert exits non-zero (gate bites) -> revert -> 0.

title: unit-test graph.py --assert — arch fitness logic
description: Synthetic god-module (fan-in AND fan-out > bottleneck_degree) / import-cycle / file>file_max each fail --assert; clean -> exit 0; [tool.structure] read + defaults merged when absent.

title: unit-test state_candidates.py — shared-param logic
description: >=2 staticmethods sharing a param (>=half), no __init__ -> flagged with param; __init__/pydantic/add_args+run -> skipped. Positive+negative.

title: unit-test data_clumps.py — maximal-set logic
description: {a,b,c} carried whole by >=4 funcs -> that maximal clump reported; support<4 / scattered -> none. Positive+negative.

title: unit-test lcom.py — LCOM4 correctness
description: Feed AST fixtures: 2 disjoint self-field method groups -> lcom4()==2 + components; cohesive class -> 1; interface-impl/abstract/all-trivial -> skipped. Positive+negative.
